Output:
# RFQ Module

This is the primary feature module. It manages vendor requests-for-quote and computes the full cost chain from FOB factory price to retail sell price.

---

## File map

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `rfq.component.ts` | Master grid component. AG Grid setup, column wiring (valueSetters/valueGetters), dialog launches, row save/delete. ~4,100 lines. |
| `helpers/equation.ts` | All margin and sell-price calculations. Injectable service (`rfqEquation`). No AG Grid imports — pure business math. |
| `helpers/api-error.ts` | HTTP error message extraction (shared across the app, lives here for historical reasons). |
| `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts` | Pure helpers (no AG Grid imports, unit-tested): `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` snapshots duty rate + container price when a "Price for Sales" cell is edited; `buildSnapshotTooltip()` formats the tooltip text. |
| `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/` | Cell renderer for the 8 `*_price_sales` columns; renders the currency value and a native `title` hover tooltip built from the row's `price_sales_snapshots`. |
| `detail-cell-renderer/` | AG Grid master-detail renderer for vendor quotes. Each RFQ item expands to show its vendor rows. |
| `addNewProduct/` | Dialog for adding a new RFQ item (full form with all fields). |
| `addNewProductGrid/` | Alternative add-product flow using a mini-grid inside a dialog. |
| `duplicate_dialog/` | Duplicates an existing RFQ item (optionally copies vendors). |
| `container_cost_dialog/` | Dialog to set container cost (USD/container); triggers freight and landed-cost recalculation. |
| `duty_rate_dialog/` | Dialog to set the duty rate. |
| `multi_quote/` | Dialog to send quote requests to multiple vendors at once. |
| `autocomplete/` | Custom AG Grid cell editor for fields with server-loaded dropdown options. |
| `custom-dropdown/` | AG Grid cell editor for RFQ Group selection with add-new inline capability. |
| `numericEditor/` | AG Grid cell editor for numeric fields (validates > 0, < 1,000,000). |
| `datepicker/` | AG Grid cell editor for date fields. |
| `layout-dropdown/` | Toolbar component for saving/loading named grid view layouts. |
| `newVendor/` | Dialog to create a new vendor and assign it to an RFQ item. |
| `vendor/` | "Select Factories" dialog — attach one or more factories to an RFQ item. Factories already on the item are shown red + unclickable so staff can't add a duplicate (see "Detail grid" below). |

Grid column definitions live in **`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`**, not in this folder. The pricing columns are wrapped into collapsible column groups by **`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq-column-groups.ts`** (see "Collapsible pricing column groups" below).

---

## Key concept: `getMasterFiledName(colId)`

Every piece of code that reads or writes to a row's data must go through `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the actual storage field name. This function iterates `this.colHeaders` (the column headers loaded from the backend) and returns the `field` value for a given `colId`.

- `colId` = stable lowercase-underscore identifier (e.g. `"fob_cost"`, `"cbm_pc"`)
- `field` = the backend storage key, which may contain spaces or special chars (e.g. `"FOB Cost"`, `"CBM/pc"`)

**Note:** The function name has a typo (`Filed` instead of `Field`). This is preserved intentionally — see AGENTS.md §11.

In `equation.ts`, the same function takes `colHeaders` as a second parameter: `getMasterFiledName(colId, colHeaders)`.

---

## Calculation pipeline

**For all pricing formulas, INCOTERM cost bases, deduction logic, and scenarios see [`docs/rfq-math.md`](../../../../docs/rfq-math.md). Only read that file when working on math — skip it otherwise.**

### Recalculation trigger chain

When a sourcing input is edited, the valueSetter in `rfq.component.ts` calls:

```
calculateLandedCost()        ← only if fob_cost / agent / duty / freight changed
recalculate_Lic_Gen()
  → setGenFobMargin          → gen_fob_sell_price
  → setGenMddpMargin         → gen_mddp_sell_price
  → setGenPoeMargin          → gen_poe_sell_price
  → setGenWhseMargin         → gen_whse_sell_price
  → setLicFobMargin          → lic_fob_sell_price
  → setLicMddpMargin         → lic_mddp_sell_price
  → setLicPoeMargin          → lic_poe_sell_price
  → setLicWhseMargin         → lic_whse_sell_price
```

All 8 setters run on every triggering edit to keep all columns in sync. Sell-price setters are only triggered when the user directly edits a sell-price cell.

---

## Price for Sales snapshot tooltips

The 8 `*_price_sales` columns are manually entered (the price sent to the sales team). A price-for-sales value is only meaningful relative to the **Duty Rate %** and **Container Price** at the time it was set — both of which drift. So when one of these cells is edited, `onCellValueChanged` calls `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` (in `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts`), which records `{ duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at }` into `params.data.price_sales_snapshots[colId]`. Clearing the cell removes that entry. The snapshot is persisted by the existing `updateRfqItem(params.data)` call (backend column `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`, TEXT/JSON).

The columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer`, which sets a native `title` tooltip from the stored snapshot so it always shows on hover. Rows with no recorded snapshot (legacy) show no tooltip. See AGENTS.md §11 for why a native tooltip is used here instead of the shared `customTooltip`.

---

## AG Grid patterns specific to this module

### Active status blockers and bilingual feedback

What changed:
Changing an RFQ row from Draft or Inactive to Active is blocked until every required launch field is present: Tech Pack Link, Case Pack, RFQ Group, Quantity, Customer, License, Description, and Delivery Location. `rfq.component.ts` passes the runtime field names from `getMasterFiledName()` into `helpers/rfq-status-validation.ts`; the validator returns `missingFields`, and both the snackbar and Status-cell native `title` tooltip show the exact missing list in English and Simplified Mandarin.

Why:
Users were blocked with a Tech Pack-only message even when another field was missing. The RFQ grid field names come from backend layout data, so the validator must receive runtime field names instead of hardcoding display labels.

Future sessions should:
When adding or removing Active-status prerequisites, update `getActiveStatusRequiredFields()` in `rfq.component.ts` and `rfq-status-validation.spec.ts`. Do not add a second ad hoc snackbar for one field; keep the missing-field list centralized so hover and click feedback stay identical.

### Silent blockers must explain the reason

What changed:
Common RFQ disabled/failure paths now surface bilingual feedback instead of only `console.error`: copy/new-round RFQ creation, vendor decline / Keep Price, factory bulk offer upload, and add-product option-load failures.

Why:
Production/vendor users can be blocked by missing group names, missing `.xlsx` files, inactive merch groups, failed dropdown loads, or backend copy guards. Console-only errors are invisible in normal use, and disabled Material buttons often need a wrapper element for tooltips to fire.

Future sessions should:
For any new disabled RFQ action, add a tooltip that names the missing prerequisite. For any caught API failure that changes user workflow, keep the `console.error` but also show a concise snackbar. If the disabled element is a Material button, wrap it in an inline element with `matTooltip`.

### Async valueSetters

Every valueSetter that calls `recalculate_Lic_Gen` is `async`. AG Grid v35 awaits the returned Promise — a rejection cancels the cell edit and reverts the value. Always:
- Return `true` on success
- Return `false` and show a snackbar on validation failure
- Do not let any exception escape (the try-catch in `recalculate_Lic_Gen` itself covers margin errors, but the outer setter must also be safe)

### Fill handle: keep drag-copy, block double-click fill-down

What changed:
The master RFQ grid still enables AG Grid fill-handle drag-copy through `cellSelection.handle: { mode: 'fill', direction: 'y', setFillValue }`. `rfq.component.ts` also installs `installFillHandleDoubleClickBlocker()` on grid ready, which catches `dblclick` events on `.ag-fill-handle` during the capture phase and stops AG Grid from auto-filling down the grid.

Why:
Double-clicking the selected-cell fill handle copied a Tech Pack Link value down the displayed grid and destroyed many row values. Users still need normal drag-to-copy, so removing the fill handle is not acceptable.

Future sessions should:
Do not "fix" this by setting `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or removing `cellSelection.handle`; that breaks drag-copy. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose the fill handle as an on/off feature and do not document a narrower double-click-only setting, so keep the event blocker unless AG Grid adds a first-class option. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag fill and double-click blocking.

### Tech Pack Link editing and opening

What changed:
The Tech Pack Link column renders through `renderTechPackCell()` instead of returning a raw `<a>` string. The cell shows the URL text plus explicit `Edit`, `Open`, and `Upload` buttons; normal clicks no longer navigate away.

Why:
When the whole cell was an anchor, users could not reliably select/edit/delete URL text because clicking the cell opened a new browser tab. The explicit buttons keep opening/uploading available while leaving AG Grid text editing accessible via double-click or the `Edit` button.

Future sessions should:
Keep Tech Pack Link navigation behind an explicit control. If changing this renderer, verify that users can still edit/delete the text value and that opening the link requires clicking `Open`.

### Native AG Grid undo

What changed:
RFQ uses AG Grid's native undo stack (`undoRedoCellEditing: true`, limit `50`), and the toolbar Undo button calls `gridApi.undoCellEditing()`. The old custom undo path that fetched all RFQ rows on every edit was removed.

Why:
AG Grid records edits, fill-handle operations, paste, delete, undo, and redo through `cellValueChanged`; the custom snapshot restore did not reliably cover fill/copy/paste and could restore stale server data.

Future sessions should:
Keep persistence in `onCellValueChanged` so undo operations save the reverted row. If adding custom edit flows, make sure they use normal AG Grid value changes (`setDataValue`, valueSetter, or editor commit) so the undo stack can see them.

### Column identity: never hardcode field names

```typescript
// Correct
params.data[this.getMasterFiledName('cbm_pc')]

// Wrong
params.data['CBM/pc']
```

### Collapsible pricing column groups

The 28 pricing columns form 8 collapsible AG Grid column groups — the cross of
{Gen, Lic} × {FOB, POE, Whse, mDDP}. "Price for Sales" stays visible when a group
is collapsed; the other columns (`columnGroupShow: 'open'`) show only when expanded.
Groups start expanded (`openByDefault: true`).

- **Grouping is a transform, not config.** `rfq.grid.config.ts` stays a **flat** array.
  `helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq-column-groups.ts` (`groupRfqPricingColumns`) wraps the
  pricing columns into `ColGroupDef`s as the **last** step when building `columnDefs`
  in `rfq.component.ts` — *after* role filtering and auto-formatters run on the flat
  list. `colHeaders` stays flat (the layout dropdown and saved-view ordering depend on
  it); only the grid's `columnDefs` is nested. It groups by the `group-header-*`
  `headerClass` marker already on each pricing column.
- **Group open/closed state persists separately from column state.** AG Grid keeps it
  in its own structure (`getColumnGroupState()`/`setColumnGroupState()` → `[{groupId,
  open}]`), so it is NOT part of `getColumnState()`. It rides along in the existing Save
  View: captured in `saveRfqViewState()` (used by both "create view" and "save current
  view") and re-applied in `applyGridViewStateForViewName()`. Stored server-side in
  `GridViewState.column_group_state` (JSONB).

### Duty rate: always use optional chaining

The duty rate can be stored as `{ title: "7.5" }` (object from autocomplete) or as a raw numeric string. Always:

```typescript
const dutyRateRaw = data[this.getMasterFiledName('duty_rate')];
const duty_rate = dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0;
```

### cellDataType: false on numeric string columns

Any column that uses `numericCellEditor` AND whose backend data is stored as strings **must** have `cellDataType: false` in its runtime setup block. Without it, AG Grid v35 infers the column type as `'text'` and issues warning `#135`, silently reverting the edit before the valueSetter is called.

```typescript
} else if (colHeaders[i].colId == "cbm_pc") {
  colHeaders[i].cellDataType = false;        // ← prevents warning #135
  colHeaders[i]['cellEditor'] = 'numericCellEditor';
  colHeaders[i].valueSetter = async (params) => { ... };
}
```

Do NOT use `cellDataType: 'number'` — that applies AG Grid's built-in number formatter and shows "invalid number" for string-valued cells loaded from the backend.

The `numericCellEditor` already enforces number-only input via `getValue()` returning `null` for any non-parseable value, so `cellDataType: false` does not open the door to bad data.

### AG Grid deprecated APIs — what NOT to use

| Removed | Replacement | Removed in |
|---|---|---|
| `params.column.userProvidedColDef` | `params.column.getColDef()` | AG Grid v35 |
| `params.columnApi` | `params.api` | AG Grid v31 |
| `getRowNodeId` | `getRowId` | AG Grid v28 |
| `observable.toPromise()` | `firstValueFrom(observable)` | RxJS 7 (deprecated) |

---

## Detail grid (vendor quotes)

Each RFQ item row expands to show vendor quotes in a detail grid managed by `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`. The detail grid:

- Loads its own column definitions from the backend (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`)
- Has its own `onCellValueChanged` that calls `updateVendor(data)` on every change
- Uses `addDetailGridInfo` / `removeDetailGridInfo` for master-detail grid registration
- The `price_code` column change in the detail grid sets `quote_accepted = true` on the vendor row
- Default-sorts by factory price ascending — see "Default sort" below

### Default sort: lowest price first, unpriced/declined last

`configureDetailColumnHeaders()` calls `applyDefaultPriceSort(colHeaders)` after building the
column defs. This makes the **price column the primary default sort** (`sort: 'asc'`,
`sortIndex: 0`); the `name` column stays as the secondary alphabetical tiebreaker (`sortIndex: 1`).

A custom comparator on the price column ranks rows so that any quote with no usable price
(blank / zero / negative `Price`) or where the factory **declined to quote** (`req_status == 1`,
set by the "pass" button in `rfq-button.component.ts`) is sunk to the bottom regardless of value;
real quotes are ordered lowest-price-first above them. The comparator reads `node.data.Price`
directly (via `isUnpricedOrDeclined()` / `toPrice()`), so it does not depend on the column's
`colId`, `field`, or `valueGetter`.

**Why `applyDefaultPriceSort` searches for the colId instead of hardcoding it:** the backend
`rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under colId **`price`** (legacy), while the
config file `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` uses `quote_price`. The helper targets whichever of the
two the layout actually provides, preferring the visible one. The first attempt (`e8ba0551`)
hardcoded `quote_price` and silently did nothing because the live column is `price`; fixed in
`b241ce79`. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one"`.

### Vendor quote rows and the duplicate-factory guard

Each item's quotes live on the master row's **`Vendors`** array (capital V — Sequelize association name); each element is `{ Vendor: { id, ... }, Price, Note, Status, quote_id, ... }`. The same `Vendor.id` may appear more than once:

- **Allowed (factory side):** the detail sub-grid "Add Quote" button lets a factory submit a second quote line for the item, as long as a note explains why the price differs. This path is intentionally **unguarded**.
- **Blocked (staff side):** the "Add Factory" → "Select Factories" dialog (`vendor/`) must not let sourcing/production/admin add a factory that is already on the item. `onBtnNewVendor()` builds `existingVendorIds` from `node.data.Vendors.map(v => v?.Vendor?.id)` and passes them as `DialogData.vendors`; the dialog renders those factories red and unclickable.

The guard is **UI-only** — there is no DB unique constraint or backend dedupe on `(RFQ item, vendor)`, and it depends on the exact capital-`V` `Vendors` casing. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Same factory twice on one RFQ item"`.

### Re-quote a stale price (orange flag + Keep Price)

When a factory's price is old, internal Sourcing/admin can right-click the detail-grid price cell → **"Request new quote"** (`getContextMenuItems`, gated to `admin`/`sourcing_manager`/`production`). That calls `itemService.requestRequote({ quote_id, priceCode })`, which sets `RFQVendor.requote_requested = true` and emails the factory. The flag drives, in **both** the staff and vendor views:

- an **orange** price cell — `cellClassRules: { 'requote-requested-cell': … }`, added to **both** the `quote_price` and legacy `price` colId branches (the live layout may serve either — same reason as the default-sort helper above);
- a **bilingual tooltip** `报价 - Quoted <Month D YYYY>` via `tooltipValueGetter`, using the original `quote_date` (which `requestRequote` leaves untouched).

The vendor clears it two ways: typing a **new price** (the `quote_price` valueSetter sets `requote_requested = false` locally; backend clears it on save) or clicking the **保留价格 - Keep Price** button (`rfq-button.component.ts` `onKeepPrice()` → `itemService.keepPrice({ quote_id })`, which clears the flag and refreshes `quote_date` to today). Vendor-facing strings are inline `中文 - English` (no i18n framework — see `AGENTS.md §11`). The backend guards both mutating endpoints with `vendorMayModifyQuote` so a vendor can't act on another factory's quote via `quote_id`.

---

## Window globals (known tech debt)

`window['itemService']` references in toolbar callbacks were resolved in commit `40b3ac9` — the service is now captured at closure-creation time in `getContextMenuItems()`.

Remaining `window` globals in `rfq.component.ts` (`window['router']`, `window['gridApi']`, `window['base64Images']`, `window['togglePageToolbar']`) are used for inter-callback communication in contexts where Angular DI is not available. **Do not add new `window['...']` references.**

# AG Grid Documentation Search Results

**Query:** clipboard paste cell ranges row groups processCellFromClipboard grouped rows
**Version:** 36.0.0
**Framework:** angular
**Total Results:** 10

---

### processCellFromClipboard

```typescript
/**
 * Allows you to process cells from the clipboard. Handy if for example you have number fields and want to block non-numbers from getting into the grid.
 * 
 * @agModule `ClipboardModule`
 */
ProcessCellFromClipboard<TData>
```

---

### pasteFromClipboard

```typescript
/**
 * Pastes the data from the Clipboard into the focused cell of the grid. If no grid cell is focused, calling this method has no effect.
 * 
 * @agModule `ClipboardModule`
 */
() => void
```

---

### processCellForClipboard

```typescript
/**
 * Allows you to process cells for the clipboard. Handy if for example you have `Date` objects that need to have a particular format if importing into Excel.
 * 
 * @agModule `ClipboardModule`
 */
ProcessCellForClipboard<TData>
```

---

### processGroupHeaderForClipboard

```typescript
/**
 * Allows you to process group header values for the clipboard.
 * 
 * @agModule `ClipboardModule`
 */
ProcessGroupHeaderForClipboard<TData>
```

---

### copySelectedRowsToClipboard

```typescript
/**
 * Copies the selected rows to the clipboard.
 * 
 * @agModule `ClipboardModule`
 */
(params?: IClipboardCopyRowsParams) => void
```

---

### suppressCopyRowsToClipboard

```typescript
/**
 * Set to `true` to copy the cell range or focused cell to the clipboard and never the selected rows.
 * 
 * @default false
 * @deprecated v32.2 Use `rowSelection.copySelectedRows` instead.
 */
boolean
```

---

For cell editing with row grouping see [Row Grouping - Editing Groups](ag-mcp://article/grouping-edit)


---

### copySelectedRangeToClipboard

```typescript
/**
 * Copies the selected ranges to the clipboard.
 * 
 * @agModule `ClipboardModule`
 */
(params?: IClipboardCopyParams) => void
```

---

### copySelectedRangeDown

```typescript
/**
 * Copies the selected range down, similar to `Ctrl + D` in Excel.
 * 
 * @agModule `ClipboardModule`
 */
() => void
```

---

### suppressCopySingleCellRanges

```typescript
/**
 * Set to `true` to copy rows instead of ranges when a range with only a single cell is selected.
 * 
 * @default false
 * @deprecated v32.2 Use `rowSelection.copySelectedRows` instead.
 */
boolean
```

# HANDOFF — Remaining open items

## RFQ Buyer Target edit gate fix — **COMMITTED & PUSHED, PR open** (updated 2026-07-07)

### 1. What this application is

Designflow is POP Creations' product-lifecycle-management web application. The Angular frontend in
`C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend` is used by sourcing, sales, design, licensing, production, and
factory/vendor users to manage RFQs, item-library records, licensing/production tracking, and related
workflow screens. This repo is the frontend only; the main PLM API lives in the sibling
`designflow-backend` service. The RFQ grid is business-critical because it stores vendor prices, buyer
targets, landed-cost inputs, sell-price calculations, and margin calculations used for quoting retailers.

Current working branch for this repo is `sandbox-albert`, pushed to
`origin/sandbox-albert`, with PR `sandbox-albert -> develop` open:
https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/141.

### 2. What we set out to do this session, and why

Albert reported that the new RFQ `"Buyer Target"` and `"Buyer Margin %"` fields were "dead" because users
could not type into either of them. These fields were part of the 16-column RFQ buyer-margin feature:
eight editable `"* Buyer Target"` dollar fields and eight calculated `"* Buyer Margin %"` fields. The
business goal is for permitted users to type a buyer target price on POP/Spruce RFQ rows, then have the
corresponding buyer margin percentage calculate from the existing RFQ cost basis without altering the
sourcing team's original sell-price/margin fields.

### 3. Current state

The frontend code fix is committed and pushed:

- Commit: `5c3f6dcd642cc336e232ecb454363970ce350446`
- Commit title: `Fix RFQ buyer target edit gating`
- Branch: `sandbox-albert`
- PR: https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/141
- GitHub check verified green: `Forbid Shared DB Bypass`, run
  https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/actions/runs/28887656252

Files changed:

- `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.ts`
- `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.spec.ts`

Behavior now:

- `"Buyer Target"` editability recognizes RFQ rows whose division arrives as:
  - `Div: "Spruce Lic"`, `Div: "Spruce non-Lic"`, or `Div: "POP Lic"`
  - `Div: { title: ... }`
  - `Div: { divCode_id: 1 | 8 | 9 }`
  - `Div: { id: 1 | 8 | 9 }`
  - lower-case/DB aliases such as `div`, `div_code_fk`, and `rfqItem_divCode_id_fk`
- POP Lic (`1`), Spruce Lic (`8`), and Spruce non-Lic (`9`) are in buyer-target scope.
- `"Buyer Margin %"` remains intentionally read-only. It is calculated from the typed Buyer Target by
  `rfq.component.ts` calling `equation.recalculate_Buyer_Margins(...)`. This is the existing spec in
  `docs/rfq-buyer-margin-plan.md`; do not make buyer-margin cells directly editable unless Albert changes
  the product requirement.

Local verification passed:

- `yarn jest src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.spec.ts --runInBand`
  - 12 tests passed.
- `yarn jest src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.spec.ts --runInBand`
  - 20 tests passed.
- `yarn jest src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.spec.ts --runInBand`
  - 58 tests passed.

Not yet done:

- PR #141 is still open and has not been merged into `develop`.
- No sandbox deployment verification was done after this fix inside this session. The fix is frontend-only and pushed,
  but the live sandbox should be checked after the PR/build/deploy path completes.

Local working tree note:

- There are pre-existing/unrelated local files still present and intentionally not committed:
  - `src/environments/environment.ts` modified
  - `proxy.conf.local.json` untracked
- These were present before this fix and were left alone.

### 4. Everything we tried that did NOT work

Initial suspicion was that both `"Buyer Target"` and `"Buyer Margin %"` were editable fields and that the grid might be
blocking both. Reading `docs/rfq-buyer-margin-plan.md` and the RFQ grid config showed that this was only partly true:
`"Buyer Target"` should be editable, but `"Buyer Margin %"` is deliberately read-only because it is a calculated result.
Changing `"Buyer Margin %"` to editable would have contradicted the shipped feature design.

The first implementation pass hardened the division helper to read `Div`, lower-case `div`,
`rfqItem_divCode_id_fk`, and `div_code_fk`. The new focused test then failed for
`isBuyerScopeRow({ rfqItem_divCode_id_fk: 8 })`: the helper found the raw alias value but only treated raw
`div_code_fk` as a numeric code. The fix was to treat the raw division value itself as the candidate code
when the value is not an object. After that, the focused helper test passed.

The first `git status` was run from `C:\repos\dflow plm`, which is only the parent folder for the six dflow repos,
not a git repository. The actual repo for this fix is `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend`.

### 5. Root causes and key findings

Root cause: `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.ts` originally checked only
`data['Div']` and only by matching division names that include `"spruce"` or `"pop"`. Some RFQ grid data paths carry
division data under aliases or as numeric ids instead of the literal `Div` display field. When AG Grid asked whether
a Buyer Target cell was editable, `isBuyerScopeRow(params.data)` returned `false`, so AG Grid never opened the numeric
editor.

Key code locations:

- `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts` around the Buyer Target setup:
  `colHeaders[i].editable = (params) => isBuyerScopeRow(params?.data);`
- `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.ts` lines 26-63 after the fix:
  `getDivisionValue(...)`, `getDivisionCodeId(...)`, `isSpruceRow(...)`, `isPopRow(...)`,
  and `isBuyerScopeRow(...)` now handle the known RFQ shapes.
- `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts` defines the buyer columns. The Buyer Margin columns have
  `editable: false` by design; the Buyer Target columns inherit editability and then get row-level gating in
  `rfq.component.ts`.

Non-obvious finding: Albert's phrase "can't type anything into either of them" sounds like two editable fields, but
the intended workflow is "type Buyer Target, read calculated Buyer Margin %." If the UI still feels wrong to Albert,
clarify whether he now wants Buyer Margin % to be manually overrideable; that is a product change, not a bug fix.

### 6. Exact next steps

1. Wait for the PR's normal frontend CI/build path and review.
   - You'll know it worked when PR #141 shows all required checks green for head SHA
     `5c3f6dcd642cc336e232ecb454363970ce350446`.

2. After PR #141 is merged/deployed to the sandbox, visually verify the RFQ grid as an admin or a user with the
   `rfq_buyer_margin` permission toggle.
   - Open an RFQ with a POP Lic, Spruce Lic, or Spruce non-Lic row.
   - Find any `"Buyer Target"` column, for example `"Gen FOB Buyer Target"`.
   - Type a number into the cell and commit the edit.
   - You'll know it worked when the cell accepts the number and the matching `"Buyer Margin %"` cells recalculate
     or remain blank according to the existing row/user visibility rules.

3. Confirm the read-only product behavior with Albert if he still expects to type directly into `"Buyer Margin %"`.
   - You'll know this is resolved when Albert confirms either:
     - current behavior is correct: Buyer Margin % is calculated only, or
     - a new requirement exists: Buyer Margin % should be manually editable/overrideable.

4. If a new manual Buyer Margin % requirement is confirmed, implement it as a new product change.
   - Update `docs/rfq-buyer-margin-plan.md` first.
   - Change the grid config and RFQ value-setter behavior deliberately.
   - Add tests proving the new write path persists the manually typed margin and does not get overwritten by Buyer
     Target recalculation unless that overwrite behavior is explicitly desired.

### 7. Constraints and gotchas in force

- Work only on `sandbox-albert` for this frontend repo; PR base is `develop`. Never push this work to `main`.
- Do not self-merge the dflow PR; Uma reviews dflow.
- Use the Git author `Albert Hazan <u2giants@users.noreply.github.com>`.
- Buyer Margin columns are read-only by current spec.
- Buyer Target is visible only to permitted users and editable only on POP/Spruce rows.
- Do not edit or commit the unrelated local `src/environments/environment.ts` or `proxy.conf.local.json` unless Albert
  explicitly asks for those.
- No backend or database change was needed for this fix.

### 8. Access and environment

- Local repo: `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend`
- Branch: `sandbox-albert`
- Remote: `https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend.git`
- PR: https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/141
- Authenticated GitHub CLI (`gh`) was available and used to inspect/watch the workflow run.
- No secrets were read, created, changed, or committed. Secrets for this environment remain in 1Password vault
  `vibe_coding` per Albert's standing instructions.

### 9. Open questions and risks

- Open question: Does Albert want `"Buyer Margin %"` to remain calculated-only, or did he expect it to be directly
  editable? As of this session, the implementation follows the existing feature spec: calculated-only.
- Risk: If live RFQ data contains a division shape not covered by the helper, a Buyer Target cell could still appear
  locked on that row. The helper now covers all shapes found in the current frontend/backend code paths during this
  session, including display names, object titles, object ids, raw aliases, and numeric division ids.
- Risk: The fix was unit-tested but not visually verified in a browser session after deployment. Do a live RFQ edit
  check after the pushed SHA reaches the sandbox.

Self-audit passed: this handoff explains the application, the session goal, exact current state, failed attempts,
root cause, concrete next steps with verification gates, constraints, access/environment, and remaining questions
for a fresh developer with no chat context.

## Performance follow-ups — **DEFERRED, earmarked for `albert-2sandbox` branch** (added 2026-06-22)

These came out of the 2026-06-22 page-load performance review. The three lower-risk
items (RFQ init `forkJoin`, reference-data caching, per-row-expansion caching) were
**already implemented** on the working branch — see `docs/performance-caching.md`.
The items below were intentionally **not** done yet; do them on `albert-2sandbox`.

### A. Server-side pagination for the RFQ `getAll` query — **DESIGNED (2026-06-23), pending developer review**

> 📄 **Full implementation design: [`docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md`](docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md)** —
> a complete cross-repo (backend + frontend) SSRM-with-server-side-grouping plan with the field
> map, filter/sort translators, Res + merch-group filterability, group/leaf query design,
> full-dataset export, file list, test plan, risks, and open questions for the reviewer.
> Product-owner decisions baked in: keep server-side grouping; Res + merch-groups must be
> filterable (carton dims need not be); export the full filtered set. **Not yet implemented** —
> review the doc before building; ship behind the dual-endpoint so `/getAllItem` stays as rollback.

**Problem:** the RFQ grid's backend `getAll` (`designflow-backend/models/lib.model.js`,
`Customer.getAll`) does an unbounded `findAll` across ~10 joined tables and returns
**all** rows (currently 3000+). It's the heaviest query behind the page.

**⚠️ The critical caveat (why this is deferred):** the RFQ grid currently uses AG
Grid's **client-side row model** — all rows are in the browser, so filtering and
sorting work across the *entire* dataset for free. If you naïvely add `LIMIT/OFFSET`
to the backend **without** also moving to AG Grid's **server-side row model**, then
filter/sort will only act on the one page currently loaded — i.e. **filtering would
silently stop working across all 3000+ rows.** That is a functional regression, not
just a perf change.

**Correct approach (the whole job):**
1. Switch the RFQ grid to the server-side row model (SSRM) — see how
   `itemLibrary` / `prod_tracking` already do this (they use SSRM today).
2. Push **filtering, sorting, and pagination to the backend** so they operate over
   the full set, and have `getAll` accept `startRow/endRow/filterModel/sortModel`.
3. Return a total row count for the grid.
4. Re-verify: Saved Views, the Division logic, default Step filter, master/detail
   expansion, export (must export the full filtered set, not just the loaded page),

payload.message: The installed type definition shows `FillOperationParams` includes `initialValues`, `values`, `currentIndex`, `currentCellValue`, and `column`, but not a row node. That explains why the existing handler falls back to scanning all `rowData`; we need to make that scan deterministic for dropdown values instead of letting the first matching group object win.

content.text: The installed type definition shows `FillOperationParams` includes `initialValues`, `values`, `currentIndex`, `currentCellValue`, and `column`, but not a row node. That explains why the existing handler falls back to scanning all `rowData`; we need to make that scan deterministic for dropdown values instead of letting the first matching group object win.

payload.message: Small correction after reading the type file: the fill callback does include `rowNode`. Good. That means we can fix this more cleanly than a global scan: use the target row and column first, and only use lookup lists when the original copied value needs to be converted back into a full dropdown object.

content.text: Small correction after reading the type file: the fill callback does include `rowNode`. Good. That means we can fix this more cleanly than a global scan: use the target row and column first, and only use lookup lists when the original copied value needs to be converted back into a full dropdown object.

payload.message: I’ve found two separate paths that can feel like the same bug in the UI: drag-fill uses `setFillValue`, while drag-select + Ctrl+C/Ctrl+V uses the clipboard processors. Since you specifically said copy/paste, I’m going to guard both, with the main fix on grouped range clipboard behavior and the fill handler cleaned up at the same time.

content.text: I’ve found two separate paths that can feel like the same bug in the UI: drag-fill uses `setFillValue`, while drag-select + Ctrl+C/Ctrl+V uses the clipboard processors. Since you specifically said copy/paste, I’m going to guard both, with the main fix on grouped range clipboard behavior and the fill handler cleaned up at the same time.

payload.message: The AG Grid implementation copies active ranges by displayed row index. In a grouped grid, that means group headers can sit inside the copied rectangle unless we intervene. I’m adding a custom clipboard send path for RFQ range copies so copied data contains only real RFQ rows, then normal paste lands row-for-row.

content.text: The AG Grid implementation copies active ranges by displayed row index. In a grouped grid, that means group headers can sit inside the copied rectangle unless we intervene. I’m adding a custom clipboard send path for RFQ range copies so copied data contains only real RFQ rows, then normal paste lands row-for-row.

payload.message: I’m going to add a `sendToClipboard` hook rather than replacing AG Grid clipboard wholesale. For normal selections it will write AG Grid’s own clipboard text unchanged; only when the active range includes group/header rows will it rebuild that clipboard text from real RFQ item rows.

content.text: I’m going to add a `sendToClipboard` hook rather than replacing AG Grid clipboard wholesale. For normal selections it will write AG Grid’s own clipboard text unchanged; only when the active range includes group/header rows will it rebuild that clipboard text from real RFQ item rows.

payload.message:
Yes, fixed and pushed.

I used the AG Grid MCP docs/version detection for this repo: AG Grid `36.0.0`, Angular.

What changed:
- RFQ range copy now skips AG Grid group/header rows when the dragged selection crosses RFQ Groups, so pasted rows line up with the real RFQ item rows.
- Drag-fill dropdown handling now resolves values through the proper dropdown lookup instead of scanning unrelated rows.
- Added tests for grouped-range copy and dropdown fill behavior.

Evidence:
- Commit: `61b8e0fe` (`Fix RFQ grouped range clipboard copy`)
- PR updated: [#141 sandbox-albert -> develop](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/141)

  - `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` — passed

- `forbid-shared-db-bypass` passed.
- Sandbox Cloud Build is still in progress at the time I checked.

I left unrelated local edits alone: two RFQ SCSS files and a separate header-height change in `rfq.component.ts`.

src/app/pages/rfq/README.md:97:Common RFQ disabled/failure paths now surface bilingual feedback instead of only `console.error`: copy/new-round RFQ creation, vendor decline / Keep Price, factory bulk offer upload, and add-product option-load failures.
src/app/pages/rfq/README.md:100:Production/vendor users can be blocked by missing group names, missing `.xlsx` files, inactive merch groups, failed dropdown loads, or backend copy guards. Console-only errors are invisible in normal use, and disabled Material buttons often need a wrapper element for tooltips to fire.
src/app/pages/rfq/README.md:112:### Fill handle: keep drag-copy, block double-click fill-down
src/app/pages/rfq/README.md:115:The master RFQ grid still enables AG Grid fill-handle drag-copy through `cellSelection.handle: { mode: 'fill', direction: 'y', setFillValue }`. `rfq.component.ts` also installs `installFillHandleDoubleClickBlocker()` on grid ready, which catches `dblclick` events on `.ag-fill-handle` during the capture phase and stops AG Grid from auto-filling down the grid.
src/app/pages/rfq/README.md:118:Double-clicking the selected-cell fill handle copied a Tech Pack Link value down the displayed grid and destroyed many row values. Users still need normal drag-to-copy, so removing the fill handle is not acceptable.
src/app/pages/rfq/README.md:121:Do not "fix" this by setting `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or removing `cellSelection.handle`; that breaks drag-copy. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose the fill handle as an on/off feature and do not document a narrower double-click-only setting, so keep the event blocker unless AG Grid adds a first-class option. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag fill and double-click blocking.
src/app/pages/rfq/README.md:140:AG Grid records edits, fill-handle operations, paste, delete, undo, and redo through `cellValueChanged`; the custom snapshot restore did not reliably cover fill/copy/paste and could restore stale server data.
src/app/pages/rfq/README.md:251:### Re-quote a stale price (orange flag + Keep Price)
src/app/pages/rfq/README.md:255:- an **orange** price cell — `cellClassRules: { 'requote-requested-cell': … }`, added to **both** the `quote_price` and legacy `price` colId branches (the live layout may serve either — same reason as the default-sort helper above);
AGENTS.md:21:When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
AGENTS.md:49:| Investigate a bug/incident | `AGENTS.md` (§14 incident log), the area's doc, `HANDOFF.md` | unrelated folder READMEs |
AGENTS.md:84:| `src/app/helpers/rfq-copy-guard.ts` | Client-side guard: prevents copying an RFQ item when Product Type / Sub-Type / Sub-Sub-Type merch groups are missing or inactive (mirrors backend `rfqCopyMerchGroupGuard.js`) |
AGENTS.md:263:| Re-quote (stale price) flag | `requote_requested` (boolean) on each `Vendors` element | Backend `RFQVendor.requote_requested`; set client-side too | `true` when Sourcing has asked the factory to refresh a stale price. Set via the "Request new quote" detail-grid context menu (internal roles only → `itemService.requestRequote`); cleared when the vendor enters a new price or clicks the **保留价格 - Keep Price** button (`itemService.keepPrice`). Drives the orange price-cell highlight (`requote-requested-cell` cellClassRules, applied to **both** the `quote_price` and legacy `price` colId branches — see §11 colId duality) and the bilingual `报价 - Quoted <date>` `tooltipValueGetter` in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`. |
AGENTS.md:453:### RFQ fill handle keeps drag-copy but blocks double-click fill-down
AGENTS.md:457:**Actually:** Drag-to-copy is intentional and must remain. The dangerous path is only the AG Grid double-click shortcut on `.ag-fill-handle`, which auto-fills down the displayed grid range. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose fill-handle on/off via `cellSelection.handle`, but no documented option to disable only the double-click shortcut.
AGENTS.md:459:**Why:** A designer accidentally double-clicked the Tech Pack Link fill handle and destroyed values down the grid. The fix in `rfq.component.ts` keeps drag fill enabled and installs a capture-phase `dblclick` listener that blocks events whose target is inside `.ag-fill-handle`.
AGENTS.md:461:**Future sessions should:** Do not set `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or remove `cellSelection.handle` just to stop double-click fill-down; that also breaks desired drag-to-copy. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag-fill config and the double-click blocker.
AGENTS.md:471:**Why:** Switching the grid to `'standard'` would change tooltip behavior for every column and the detail grid, and would require reworking the shared `CustomTooltipComponent` truncation gating (high regression risk per the AG Grid incidents in §14). A native `title` set by a dedicated renderer always shows on hover for just these 8 columns, fully isolated. The renderer also clears `tooltipField` on those columns so the AG tooltip does not double up.
AGENTS.md:495:**Do not change because:** "Fixing" one string to a `| translate` key while the framework is uninitialized renders the raw key (e.g. `RFQ.DECLINE`). Either keep the inline bilingual form, or stand up the full ngx-translate pipeline in one coordinated change — don't half-migrate. New vendor-facing copy should follow the `中文 - English` convention.
AGENTS.md:603:**Actually:** They are memoized via `getCached()` (`shareReplay`, errors not cached, subscribers get a shallow array copy). # RFQ Module

This is the primary feature module. It manages vendor requests-for-quote and computes the full cost chain from FOB factory price to retail sell price.

---

## File map

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `rfq.component.ts` | Master grid component. AG Grid setup, column wiring (valueSetters/valueGetters), dialog launches, row save/delete. ~4,100 lines. |
| `helpers/equation.ts` | All margin and sell-price calculations. Injectable service (`rfqEquation`). No AG Grid imports — pure business math. |
| `helpers/api-error.ts` | HTTP error message extraction (shared across the app, lives here for historical reasons). |
| `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts` | Pure helpers (no AG Grid imports, unit-tested): `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` snapshots duty rate + container price when a "Price for Sales" cell is edited; `buildSnapshotTooltip()` formats the tooltip text. |
| `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/` | Cell renderer for the 8 `*_price_sales` columns; renders the currency value and a native `title` hover tooltip built from the row's `price_sales_snapshots`. |
| `detail-cell-renderer/` | AG Grid master-detail renderer for vendor quotes. Each RFQ item expands to show its vendor rows. |
| `addNewProduct/` | Dialog for adding a new RFQ item (full form with all fields). |
| `addNewProductGrid/` | Alternative add-product flow using a mini-grid inside a dialog. |
| `duplicate_dialog/` | Duplicates an existing RFQ item (optionally copies vendors). |
| `container_cost_dialog/` | Dialog to set container cost (USD/container); triggers freight and landed-cost recalculation. |
| `duty_rate_dialog/` | Dialog to set the duty rate. |
| `multi_quote/` | Dialog to send quote requests to multiple vendors at once. |
| `autocomplete/` | Custom AG Grid cell editor for fields with server-loaded dropdown options. |
| `custom-dropdown/` | AG Grid cell editor for RFQ Group selection with add-new inline capability. |
| `numericEditor/` | AG Grid cell editor for numeric fields (validates > 0, < 1,000,000). |
| `datepicker/` | AG Grid cell editor for date fields. |
| `layout-dropdown/` | Toolbar component for saving/loading named grid view layouts. |
| `newVendor/` | Dialog to create a new vendor and assign it to an RFQ item. |
| `vendor/` | "Select Factories" dialog — attach one or more factories to an RFQ item. Factories already on the item are shown red + unclickable so staff can't add a duplicate (see "Detail grid" below). |

Grid column definitions live in **`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`**, not in this folder. The pricing columns are wrapped into collapsible column groups by **`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq-column-groups.ts`** (see "Collapsible pricing column groups" below).

---

## Key concept: `getMasterFiledName(colId)`

Every piece of code that reads or writes to a row's data must go through `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the actual storage field name. This function iterates `this.colHeaders` (the column headers loaded from the backend) and returns the `field` value for a given `colId`.

- `colId` = stable lowercase-underscore identifier (e.g. `"fob_cost"`, `"cbm_pc"`)
- `field` = the backend storage key, which may contain spaces or special chars (e.g. `"FOB Cost"`, `"CBM/pc"`)

**Note:** The function name has a typo (`Filed` instead of `Field`). This is preserved intentionally — see AGENTS.md §11.

In `equation.ts`, the same function takes `colHeaders` as a second parameter: `getMasterFiledName(colId, colHeaders)`.

---

## Calculation pipeline

**For all pricing formulas, INCOTERM cost bases, deduction logic, and scenarios see [`docs/rfq-math.md`](../../../../docs/rfq-math.md). Only read that file when working on math — skip it otherwise.**

### Recalculation trigger chain

When a sourcing input is edited, the valueSetter in `rfq.component.ts` calls:

```
calculateLandedCost()        ← only if fob_cost / agent / duty / freight changed
recalculate_Lic_Gen()
  → setGenFobMargin          → gen_fob_sell_price
  → setGenMddpMargin         → gen_mddp_sell_price
  → setGenPoeMargin          → gen_poe_sell_price
  → setGenWhseMargin         → gen_whse_sell_price
  → setLicFobMargin          → lic_fob_sell_price
  → setLicMddpMargin         → lic_mddp_sell_price
  → setLicPoeMargin          → lic_poe_sell_price
  → setLicWhseMargin         → lic_whse_sell_price
```

All 8 setters run on every triggering edit to keep all columns in sync. Sell-price setters are only triggered when the user directly edits a sell-price cell.

---

## Price for Sales snapshot tooltips

The 8 `*_price_sales` columns are manually entered (the price sent to the sales team). A price-for-sales value is only meaningful relative to the **Duty Rate %** and **Container Price** at the time it was set — both of which drift. So when one of these cells is edited, `onCellValueChanged` calls `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` (in `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts`), which records `{ duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at }` into `params.data.price_sales_snapshots[colId]`. Clearing the cell removes that entry. The snapshot is persisted by the existing `updateRfqItem(params.data)` call (backend column `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`, TEXT/JSON).

The columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer`, which sets a native `title` tooltip from the stored snapshot so it always shows on hover. Rows with no recorded snapshot (legacy) show no tooltip. See AGENTS.md §11 for why a native tooltip is used here instead of the shared `customTooltip`.

---

## AG Grid patterns specific to this module

### Active status blockers and bilingual feedback

What changed:
Changing an RFQ row from Draft or Inactive to Active is blocked until every required launch field is present: Tech Pack Link, Case Pack, RFQ Group, Quantity, Customer, License, Description, and Delivery Location. `rfq.component.ts` passes the runtime field names from `getMasterFiledName()` into `helpers/rfq-status-validation.ts`; the validator returns `missingFields`, and both the snackbar and Status-cell native `title` tooltip show the exact missing list in English and Simplified Mandarin.

Why:
Users were blocked with a Tech Pack-only message even when another field was missing. The RFQ grid field names come from backend layout data, so the validator must receive runtime field names instead of hardcoding display labels.

Future sessions should:
When adding or removing Active-status prerequisites, update `getActiveStatusRequiredFields()` in `rfq.component.ts` and `rfq-status-validation.spec.ts`. Do not add a second ad hoc snackbar for one field; keep the missing-field list centralized so hover and click feedback stay identical.

### Silent blockers must explain the reason

What changed:
Common RFQ disabled/failure paths now surface bilingual feedback instead of only `console.error`: copy/new-round RFQ creation, vendor decline / Keep Price, factory bulk offer upload, and add-product option-load failures.

Why:
Production/vendor users can be blocked by missing group names, missing `.xlsx` files, inactive merch groups, failed dropdown loads, or backend copy guards. Console-only errors are invisible in normal use, and disabled Material buttons often need a wrapper element for tooltips to fire.

Future sessions should:
For any new disabled RFQ action, add a tooltip that names the missing prerequisite. For any caught API failure that changes user workflow, keep the `console.error` but also show a concise snackbar. If the disabled element is a Material button, wrap it in an inline element with `matTooltip`.

### Async valueSetters

Every valueSetter that calls `recalculate_Lic_Gen` is `async`. AG Grid v35 awaits the returned Promise — a rejection cancels the cell edit and reverts the value. Always:
- Return `true` on success
- Return `false` and show a snackbar on validation failure
- Do not let any exception escape (the try-catch in `recalculate_Lic_Gen` itself covers margin errors, but the outer setter must also be safe)

### Fill handle: keep drag-copy, block double-click fill-down

What changed:
The master RFQ grid still enables AG Grid fill-handle drag-copy through `cellSelection.handle: { mode: 'fill', direction: 'y', setFillValue }`. `rfq.component.ts` also installs `installFillHandleDoubleClickBlocker()` on grid ready, which catches `dblclick` events on `.ag-fill-handle` during the capture phase and stops AG Grid from auto-filling down the grid.

Why:
Double-clicking the selected-cell fill handle copied a Tech Pack Link value down the displayed grid and destroyed many row values. Users still need normal drag-to-copy, so removing the fill handle is not acceptable.

Future sessions should:
Do not "fix" this by setting `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or removing `cellSelection.handle`; that breaks drag-copy. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose the fill handle as an on/off feature and do not document a narrower double-click-only setting, so keep the event blocker unless AG Grid adds a first-class option. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag fill and double-click blocking.

### Tech Pack Link editing and opening

What changed:
The Tech Pack Link column renders through `renderTechPackCell()` instead of returning a raw `<a>` string. The cell shows the URL text plus explicit `Edit`, `Open`, and `Upload` buttons; normal clicks no longer navigate away.

Why:
When the whole cell was an anchor, users could not reliably select/edit/delete URL text because clicking the cell opened a new browser tab. The explicit buttons keep opening/uploading available while leaving AG Grid text editing accessible via double-click or the `Edit` button.

Future sessions should:
Keep Tech Pack Link navigation behind an explicit control. If changing this renderer, verify that users can still edit/delete the text value and that opening the link requires clicking `Open`.

### Native AG Grid undo

What changed:
RFQ uses AG Grid's native undo stack (`undoRedoCellEditing: true`, limit `50`), and the toolbar Undo button calls `gridApi.undoCellEditing()`. The old custom undo path that fetched all RFQ rows on every edit was removed.

Why:
AG Grid records edits, fill-handle operations, paste, delete, undo, and redo through `cellValueChanged`; the custom snapshot restore did not reliably cover fill/copy/paste and could restore stale server data.

Future sessions should:
Keep persistence in `onCellValueChanged` so undo operations save the reverted row. If adding custom edit flows, make sure they use normal AG Grid value changes (`setDataValue`, valueSetter, or editor commit) so the undo stack can see them.

### Column identity: never hardcode field names

```typescript
// Correct
params.data[this.getMasterFiledName('cbm_pc')]

// Wrong
params.data['CBM/pc']
```

### Collapsible pricing column groups

The 28 pricing columns form 8 collapsible AG Grid column groups — the cross of
{Gen, Lic} × {FOB, POE, Whse, mDDP}. "Price for Sales" stays visible when a group
is collapsed; the other columns (`columnGroupShow: 'open'`) show only when expanded.
Groups start expanded (`openByDefault: true`).

- **Grouping is a transform, not config.** `rfq.grid.config.ts` stays a **flat** array.
  `helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq-column-groups.ts` (`groupRfqPricingColumns`) wraps the
  pricing columns into `ColGroupDef`s as the **last** step when building `columnDefs`
  in `rfq.component.ts` — *after* role filtering and auto-formatters run on the flat
  list. `colHeaders` stays flat (the layout dropdown and saved-view ordering depend on
  it); only the grid's `columnDefs` is nested. It groups by the `group-header-*`
  `headerClass` marker already on each pricing column.
- **Group open/closed state persists separately from column state.** AG Grid keeps it
  in its own structure (`getColumnGroupState()`/`setColumnGroupState()` → `[{groupId,
  open}]`), so it is NOT part of `getColumnState()`. It rides along in the existing Save
  View: captured in `saveRfqViewState()` (used by both "create view" and "save current
  view") and re-applied in `applyGridViewStateForViewName()`. Stored server-side in
  `GridViewState.column_group_state` (JSONB).

### Duty rate: always use optional chaining

The duty rate can be stored as `{ title: "7.5" }` (object from autocomplete) or as a raw numeric string. Always:

```typescript
const dutyRateRaw = data[this.getMasterFiledName('duty_rate')];
const duty_rate = dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0;
```

### cellDataType: false on numeric string columns

Any column that uses `numericCellEditor` AND whose backend data is stored as strings **must** have `cellDataType: false` in its runtime setup block. Without it, AG Grid v35 infers the column type as `'text'` and issues warning `#135`, silently reverting the edit before the valueSetter is called.

```typescript
} else if (colHeaders[i].colId == "cbm_pc") {
  colHeaders[i].cellDataType = false;        // ← prevents warning #135
  colHeaders[i]['cellEditor'] = 'numericCellEditor';
  colHeaders[i].valueSetter = async (params) => { ... };
}
```

Do NOT use `cellDataType: 'number'` — that applies AG Grid's built-in number formatter and shows "invalid number" for string-valued cells loaded from the backend.

The `numericCellEditor` already enforces number-only input via `getValue()` returning `null` for any non-parseable value, so `cellDataType: false` does not open the door to bad data.

### AG Grid deprecated APIs — what NOT to use

| Removed | Replacement | Removed in |
|---|---|---|
| `params.column.userProvidedColDef` | `params.column.getColDef()` | AG Grid v35 |
| `params.columnApi` | `params.api` | AG Grid v31 |
| `getRowNodeId` | `getRowId` | AG Grid v28 |
| `observable.toPromise()` | `firstValueFrom(observable)` | RxJS 7 (deprecated) |

---

## Detail grid (vendor quotes)

Each RFQ item row expands to show vendor quotes in a detail grid managed by `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`. The detail grid:

- Loads its own column definitions from the backend (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`)
- Has its own `onCellValueChanged` that calls `updateVendor(data)` on every change
- Uses `addDetailGridInfo` / `removeDetailGridInfo` for master-detail grid registration
- The `price_code` column change in the detail grid sets `quote_accepted = true` on the vendor row
- Default-sorts by factory price ascending — see "Default sort" below

### Default sort: lowest price first, unpriced/declined last

`configureDetailColumnHeaders()` calls `applyDefaultPriceSort(colHeaders)` after building the
column defs. This makes the **price column the primary default sort** (`sort: 'asc'`,
`sortIndex: 0`); the `name` column stays as the secondary alphabetical tiebreaker (`sortIndex: 1`).

A custom comparator on the price column ranks rows so that any quote with no usable price
(blank / zero / negative `Price`) or where the factory **declined to quote** (`req_status == 1`,
set by the "pass" button in `rfq-button.component.ts`) is sunk to the bottom regardless of value;
real quotes are ordered lowest-price-first above them. The comparator reads `node.data.Price`
directly (via `isUnpricedOrDeclined()` / `toPrice()`), so it does not depend on the column's
`colId`, `field`, or `valueGetter`.

**Why `applyDefaultPriceSort` searches for the colId instead of hardcoding it:** the backend
`rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under colId **`price`** (legacy), while the
config file `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` uses `quote_price`. The helper targets whichever of the
two the layout actually provides, preferring the visible one. The first attempt (`e8ba0551`)
hardcoded `quote_price` and silently did nothing because the live column is `price`; fixed in
`b241ce79`. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one"`.

### Vendor quote rows and the duplicate-factory guard

Each item's quotes live on the master row's **`Vendors`** array (capital V — Sequelize association name); each element is `{ Vendor: { id, ... }, Price, Note, Status, quote_id, ... }`. The same `Vendor.id` may appear more than once:

- **Allowed (factory side):** the detail sub-grid "Add Quote" button lets a factory submit a second quote line for the item, as long as a note explains why the price differs. This path is intentionally **unguarded**.
- **Blocked (staff side):** the "Add Factory" → "Select Factories" dialog (`vendor/`) must not let sourcing/production/admin add a factory that is already on the item. `onBtnNewVendor()` builds `existingVendorIds` from `node.data.Vendors.map(v => v?.Vendor?.id)` and passes them as `DialogData.vendors`; the dialog renders those factories red and unclickable.

The guard is **UI-only** — there is no DB unique constraint or backend dedupe on `(RFQ item, vendor)`, and it depends on the exact capital-`V` `Vendors` casing. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Same factory twice on one RFQ item"`.

### Re-quote a stale price (orange flag + Keep Price)

When a factory's price is old, internal Sourcing/admin can right-click the detail-grid price cell → **"Request new quote"** (`getContextMenuItems`, gated to `admin`/`sourcing_manager`/`production`). That calls `itemService.requestRequote({ quote_id, priceCode })`, which sets `RFQVendor.requote_requested = true` and emails the factory. The flag drives, in **both** the staff and vendor views:

- an **orange** price cell — `cellClassRules: { 'requote-requested-cell': … }`, added to **both** the `quote_price` and legacy `price` colId branches (the live layout may serve either — same reason as the default-sort helper above);
- a **bilingual tooltip** `报价 - Quoted <Month D YYYY>` via `tooltipValueGetter`, using the original `quote_date` (which `requestRequote` leaves untouched).

The vendor clears it two ways: typing a **new price** (the `quote_price` valueSetter sets `requote_requested = false` locally; backend clears it on save) or clicking the **保留价格 - Keep Price** button (`rfq-button.component.ts` `onKeepPrice()` → `itemService.keepPrice({ quote_id })`, which clears the flag and refreshes `quote_date` to today). Vendor-facing strings are inline `中文 - English` (no i18n framework — see `AGENTS.md §11`). The backend guards both mutating endpoints with `vendorMayModifyQuote` so a vendor can't act on another factory's quote via `quote_id`.

---

## Window globals (known tech debt)

`window['itemService']` references in toolbar callbacks were resolved in commit `40b3ac9` — the service is now captured at closure-creation time in `getContextMenuItems()`.

Remaining `window` globals in `rfq.component.ts` (`window['router']`, `window['gridApi']`, `window['base64Images']`, `window['togglePageToolbar']`) are used for inter-callback communication in contexts where Angular DI is not available. **Do not add new `window['...']` references.**

## RFQ Buyer Target edit gate fix — **COMMITTED & PUSHED, PR open** (updated 2026-07-07)

### 1. What this application is

Designflow is POP Creations' product-lifecycle-management web application. The Angular frontend in
`C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend` is used by sourcing, sales, design, licensing, production, and
factory/vendor users to manage RFQs, item-library records, licensing/production tracking, and related
workflow screens. This repo is the frontend only; the main PLM API lives in the sibling
`designflow-backend` service. The RFQ grid is business-critical because it stores vendor prices, buyer
targets, landed-cost inputs, sell-price calculations, and margin calculations used for quoting retailers.

Current working branch for this repo is `sandbox-albert`, pushed to
`origin/sandbox-albert`, with PR `sandbox-albert -> develop` open:
https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/141.

### 2. What we set out to do this session, and why

Albert reported that the new RFQ `"Buyer Target"` and `"Buyer Margin %"` fields were "dead" because users
could not type into either of them. These fields were part of the 16-column RFQ buyer-margin feature:
eight editable `"* Buyer Target"` dollar fields and eight calculated `"* Buyer Margin %"` fields. The
business goal is for permitted users to type a buyer target price on POP/Spruce RFQ rows, then have the
corresponding buyer margin percentage calculate from the existing RFQ cost basis without altering the
sourcing team's original sell-price/margin fields.

### 3. Current state

The frontend code fix is committed and pushed:

- Commit: `5c3f6dcd642cc336e232ecb454363970ce350446`
- Commit title: `Fix RFQ buyer target edit gating`
- Branch: `sandbox-albert`
- PR: https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/141
- GitHub check verified green: `Forbid Shared DB Bypass`, run
  https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/actions/runs/28887656252

Files changed:

- `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.ts`
- `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.spec.ts`

Behavior now:

- `"Buyer Target"` editability recognizes RFQ rows whose division arrives as:
  - `Div: "Spruce Lic"`, `Div: "Spruce non-Lic"`, or `Div: "POP Lic"`
  - `Div: { title: ... }`
  - `Div: { divCode_id: 1 | 8 | 9 }`
  - `Div: { id: 1 | 8 | 9 }`
  - lower-case/DB aliases such as `div`, `div_code_fk`, and `rfqItem_divCode_id_fk`
- POP Lic (`1`), Spruce Lic (`8`), and Spruce non-Lic (`9`) are in buyer-target scope.
- `"Buyer Margin %"` remains intentionally read-only. It is calculated from the typed Buyer Target by
  `rfq.component.ts` calling `equation.recalculate_Buyer_Margins(...)`. This is the existing spec in
  `docs/rfq-buyer-margin-plan.md`; do not make buyer-margin cells directly editable unless Albert changes

- These were present before this fix and were left alone.

### 4. Everything we tried that did NOT work

Initial suspicion was that both `"Buyer Target"` and `"Buyer Margin %"` were editable fields and that the grid might be
blocking both. Reading `docs/rfq-buyer-margin-plan.md` and the RFQ grid config showed that this was only partly true:
`"Buyer Target"` should be editable, but `"Buyer Margin %"` is deliberately read-only because it is a calculated result.
Changing `"Buyer Margin %"` to editable would have contradicted the shipped feature design.

The first implementation pass hardened the division helper to read `Div`, lower-case `div`,
`rfqItem_divCode_id_fk`, and `div_code_fk`. The new focused test then failed for
`isBuyerScopeRow({ rfqItem_divCode_id_fk: 8 })`: the helper found the raw alias value but only treated raw
`div_code_fk` as a numeric code. The fix was to treat the raw division value itself as the candidate code
when the value is not an object. After that, the focused helper test passed.

The first `git status` was run from `C:\repos\dflow plm`, which is only the parent folder for the six dflow repos,
not a git repository. The actual repo for this fix is `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend`.

### 5. Root causes and key findings

Root cause: `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.ts` originally checked only
`data['Div']` and only by matching division names that include `"spruce"` or `"pop"`. Some RFQ grid data paths carry
division data under aliases or as numeric ids instead of the literal `Div` display field. When AG Grid asked whether
a Buyer Target cell was editable, `isBuyerScopeRow(params.data)` returned `false`, so AG Grid never opened the numeric
editor.

Key code locations:

- `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts` around the Buyer Target setup:
  `colHeaders[i].editable = (params) => isBuyerScopeRow(params?.data);`
- `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.ts` lines 26-63 after the fix:
  `getDivisionValue(...)`, `getDivisionCodeId(...)`, `isSpruceRow(...)`, `isPopRow(...)`,
  and `isBuyerScopeRow(...)` now handle the known RFQ shapes.
- `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts` defines the buyer columns. The Buyer Margin columns have
  `editable: false` by design; the Buyer Target columns inherit editability and then get row-level gating in
  `rfq.component.ts`.

Non-obvious finding: Albert's phrase "can't type anything into either of them" sounds like two editable fields, but
the intended workflow is "type Buyer Target, read calculated Buyer Margin %." If the UI still feels wrong to Albert,
clarify whether he now wants Buyer Margin % to be manually overrideable; that is a product change, not a bug fix.

### 6. Exact next steps

1. Wait for the PR's normal frontend CI/build path and review.
   - You'll know it worked when PR #141 shows all required checks green for head SHA
     `5c3f6dcd642cc336e232ecb454363970ce350446`.

2. After PR #141 is merged/deployed to the sandbox, visually verify the RFQ grid as an admin or a user with the
   `rfq_buyer_margin` permission toggle.
   - Open an RFQ with a POP Lic, Spruce Lic, or Spruce non-Lic row.
   - Find any `"Buyer Target"` column, for example `"Gen FOB Buyer Target"`.
   - Type a number into the cell and commit the edit.
   - You'll know it worked when the cell accepts the number and the matching `"Buyer Margin %"` cells recalculate
     or remain blank according to the existing row/user visibility rules.

3. Confirm the read-only product behavior with Albert if he still expects to type directly into `"Buyer Margin %"`.
   - You'll know this is resolved when Albert confirms either:
     - current behavior is correct: Buyer Margin % is calculated only, or
     - a new requirement exists: Buyer Margin % should be manually editable/overrideable.

4. If a new manual Buyer Margin % requirement is confirmed, implement it as a new product change.
   - Update `docs/rfq-buyer-margin-plan.md` first.
   - Change the grid config and RFQ value-setter behavior deliberately.
   - Add tests proving the new write path persists the manually typed margin and does not get overwritten by Buyer
     Target recalculation unless that overwrite behavior is explicitly desired.

### 7. Constraints and gotchas in force

- Work only on `sandbox-albert` for this frontend repo; PR base is `develop`. Never push this work to `main`.
- Do not self-merge the dflow PR; Uma reviews dflow.
- Use the Git author `Albert Hazan <u2giants@users.noreply.github.com>`.
- Buyer Margin columns are read-only by current spec.
- Buyer Target is visible only to permitted users and editable only on POP/Spruce rows.
- Do not edit or commit the unrelated local `src/environments/environment.ts` or `proxy.conf.local.json` unless Albert
  explicitly asks for those.
- No backend or database change was needed for this fix.

### 8. Access and environment

- Local repo: `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend`
- Branch: `sandbox-albert`
- Remote: `https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend.git`
- PR: https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/141
- Authenticated GitHub CLI (`gh`) was available and used to inspect/watch the workflow run.
- No secrets were read, created, changed, or committed. Secrets for this environment remain in 1Password vault
  `vibe_coding` per Albert's standing instructions.

### 9. Open questions and risks

- Open question: Does Albert want `"Buyer Margin %"` to remain calculated-only, or did he expect it to be directly
  editable? As of this session, the implementation follows the existing feature spec: calculated-only.
- Risk: If live RFQ data contains a division shape not covered by the helper, a Buyer Target cell could still appear
  locked on that row. The helper now covers all shapes found in the current frontend/backend code paths during this
  session, including display names, object titles, object ids, raw aliases, and numeric division ids.
- Risk: The fix was unit-tested but not visually verified in a browser session after deployment. Do a live RFQ edit
  check after the pushed SHA reaches the sandbox.

Self-audit passed: this handoff explains the application, the session goal, exact current state, failed attempts,
root cause, concrete next steps with verification gates, constraints, access/environment, and remaining questions
for a fresh developer with no chat context.

---

## Performance follow-ups — **DEFERRED, earmarked for `albert-2sandbox` branch** (added 2026-06-22)

These came out of the 2026-06-22 page-load performance review. The three lower-risk
items (RFQ init `forkJoin`, reference-data caching, per-row-expansion caching) were
**already implemented** on the working branch — see `docs/performance-caching.md`.
The items below were intentionally **not** done yet; do them on `albert-2sandbox`.

### A. Server-side pagination for the RFQ `getAll` query — **DESIGNED (2026-06-23), pending developer review**

> 📄 **Full implementation design: [`docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md`](docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md)** —
> a complete cross-repo (backend + frontend) SSRM-with-server-side-grouping plan with the field
> map, filter/sort translators, Res + merch-group filterability, group/leaf query design,
> full-dataset export, file list, test plan, risks, and open questions for the reviewer.
> Product-owner decisions baked in: keep server-side grouping; Res + merch-groups must be
> filterable (carton dims need not be); export the full filtered set. **Not yet implemented** —
> review the doc before building; ship behind the dual-endpoint so `/getAllItem` stays as rollback.

**Problem:** the RFQ grid's backend `getAll` (`designflow-backend/models/lib.model.js`,
`Customer.getAll`) does an unbounded `findAll` across ~10 joined tables and returns
**all** rows (currently 3000+). It's the heaviest query behind the page.

**⚠️ The critical caveat (why this is deferred):** the RFQ grid currently uses AG
Grid's **client-side row model** — all rows are in the browser, so filtering and
sorting work across the *entire* dataset for free. If you naïvely add `LIMIT/OFFSET`
to the backend **without** also moving to AG Grid's **server-side row model**, then
filter/sort will only act on the one page currently loaded — i.e. **filtering would
silently stop working across all 3000+ rows.** That is a functional regression, not
just a perf change.

**Correct approach (the whole job):**
1. Switch the RFQ grid to the server-side row model (SSRM) — see how
   `itemLibrary` / `prod_tracking` already do this (they use SSRM today).
2. Push **filtering, sorting, and pagination to the backend** so they operate over
   the full set, and have `getAll` accept `startRow/endRow/filterModel/sortModel`.
3. Return a total row count for the grid.
4. Re-verify: Saved Views, the Division logic, default Step filter, master/detail
   expansion, export (must export the full filtered set, not just the loaded page),
   and cell notes.

**Do not ship a bare `LIMIT` on `getAll`.** Either do the full SSRM migration or leave it.

### B. Adopt `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` + precompute template getters

**Problem:** only ~8 of ~278 components use OnPush. Grid pages (rfq, itemLibrary,
prod_tracking, etc.) use default change detection with method/getter bindings that
re-run every CD cycle (e.g. `valueGetter` doing `allMerchGroups.find(...)` per cell,
template getters that rebuild arrays).

**Approach (incremental, per-component — do NOT flip globally):**
- Convert one heavy component at a time to `OnPush`; drive updates through the
  already-injected `ChangeDetectorRef` (`markForCheck()`), and use immutable updates
  (new array/object references) instead of in-place mutation.
- Precompute template getter results into fields when the source data changes.
- **Risk:** OnPush changes *when* the view updates — anything relying on mutate-in-place
  will silently stop refreshing. Each component needs manual testing after conversion.

### C. N+1 / row-by-row writes → `bulkCreate` — **AUDIT-FIRST process defined (2026-06-23)**

> 📄 **Audit template + staged process + hotspot inventory:
> [`designflow-data-syncing/docs/bulkcreate-migration-audit.md`](../designflow-data-syncing/docs/bulkcreate-migration-audit.md)**
> (lives in the repo where the work happens). It defines a per-hotspot fill-in audit checklist and a
> staged process: **(0) fix error visibility → (1) per-hotspot audit incl. the conditional-update
> decision → (2) verify/add the DB unique constraint → (3) baseline + tests + scheduler validation
> in non-prod → (4) chunked `bulkCreate` in a transaction → (5) parity check → next service.**
> Migrate **one service at a time**; first service is a pattern-setter (merch-group sync), NOT the
> riskiest (`getProdOrderDetail`).

This is primarily a **backend / sync-service** concern, listed here so the frontend
handoff points at it. Row-by-row `INSERT`/`UPDATE` loops (often with a per-row
`SELECT` first) should become batched `bulkCreate(rows, { updateOnDuplicate: [...] })`
or batched `findAll({ where: { [Op.in]: keys } })`. Hotspots identified:
`designflow-data-syncing` sync loops (e.g. `getProdOrderDetail`, merch-group sync,
prepack associations) and `designflow-tracking` `Box.addSamples`.

**Why "investigate further", not "just do it":**
- Each site needs a real **unique constraint** + an explicit `updateOnDuplicate`
  column list — wrong list either clobbers kept data or silently skips updates.

Removing `.title` requires re-evaluating the equation in `equation.ts` on every margin calculation (and `equation.ts` runs hot — 16 margin setters per row edit).

443: **Looks like:** A bug — the cell edit sometimes cancels even when the user typed a valid value.
444: 
445: **Actually:** AG Grid v35 awaits an async `valueSetter`'s returned Promise. A rejected Promise (caused by any uncaught throw inside the setter) cancels the edit and reverts the cell to its previous value.

447: **Why:** `equation.ts` previously accessed `params.column.userProvidedColDef.colId` (removed in AG Grid v35) inside every margin setter, causing a `TypeError` on every edit. Fixed in `daf2bf9`.

449: **Do not change because:** Any uncaught exception inside an async valueSetter will silently revert the cell. All new valueSetters must be wrapped in try-catch, and all AG Grid API calls inside them must use current (non-deprecated) APIs.

AG Grid v35.1 docs expose fill-handle on/off via `cellSelection.handle`, but no documented option to disable only the double-click shortcut.

693: 
694: **What happened:** A hybrid AI E2E pass (drive the live app like a human + read the code) surfaced ~9 real defects across Sample Tracking, Item Detail, RFQ, Comments, and Admin. Two were blockers: (1) the Sample Tracking grid mounted in a 0px-height container — all rows present in the DOM but invisible; (2) "Request Quote" on Item Detail opened an empty, `disableClose` dialog (NG0201: `StdService` not provided in `ItemDetailModule`) that soft-locked the screen.

RFQ Gen FOB calc verified unchanged (22/22).

699: - **AG Grid stale-deps drift recurred** (same class as the 2026-06-13 entry): local `node_modules` lagged `package.json` and broke `ng serve` until `yarn install`. Mid-audit the repo also jumped to AG Grid **v36** — verify the *installed* version before asserting it in docs.
700: - **Local E2E against the deployed API needs a dev-server proxy bridge** — the deployed BFF's CORS allowlist rejects a `localhost:4200` origin, so a local frontend can't log in directly. Serve with `--configuration sandbox --proxy-config` and relative API URLs (revert after).
701: - The reusable tester lives in `.claude/skills/designflow-e2e-tester/` (auto-discovered by Claude Code; mirrored across all designflow repos).
