703: ### 2026-06-15 — Stale `index.html` cache served an old frontend (empty RFQ sub-grids after deploy)
704: 
705: **What happened:** After a backend change to how RFQ factory quotes load (a `develop` "RFQ performance" refactor → lazy per-row vendor loading via a new `getRfqItemVendors` endpoint), users saw empty detail sub-grids even though the `Res` column showed quote counts, and the page looked unchanged after frontend deploys.
706: 
707: **Root cause:** `nginx.conf` served `index.html` with a 30-day cache (the catch-all `location ~* \.(js|css|pdf|html|swf)$ { expires 30d }` block included `html`). Browsers kept running a **cached old frontend** that loads quotes the old (eager) way; against the new lazy backend it rendered empty. The deployed app was actually correct and matched — the browser was stale.
708: 
709: **Recovery:** Fixed `nginx.conf` (`7e9ca2d7`): `index.html` is now `Cache-Control: no-cache` via an exact-match `location = /index.html` block (security headers restated there since an `add_header` in a location replaces inherited ones), and `html` was removed from the long-cache bucket; content-hashed JS/CSS stay long-cached. Currently-cached browsers need one hard refresh; afterward, deploys reach everyone immediately. The triggering backend merge was reverted on `sandbox-albert` (see `designflow-backend` §14). Detail: `docs/deployment.md → Static asset caching`.
710: 
711: **Rule added:** Never long-cache `index.html` (or any SPA entry HTML) — cache only content-hashed assets. Any rollout that changes a frontend↔backend data contract (e.g. lazy loading) will break browsers pinned to a stale entry page until the cache fix is deployed AND they reload.

715: ### 2025 — RFQ calculations silently broken by `aggFunc` on Date Modified
716: 
717: **What happened:** Adding `aggFunc: 'max'` to the Date Modified column (commit `43db88d`) activated AG Grid Enterprise's full aggregation pipeline. All group rows lost `params.data` (became `undefined`) in every valueSetter and valueGetter.
718: 
719: **Impact:** Landed cost, freight, and all 16 margin calculations silently returned wrong values or threw for any group row. Users saw blank cells.
720: 
721: **Root cause:** AG Grid Enterprise activates aggregation for the entire grid (not just the column) when any column has an `aggFunc`. Group rows then use aggregated values, not `params.data`.
722: 
723: **Recovery:** Removed `aggFunc` entirely from `applyAutoFormatters()`. Group ordering is handled by `postSortRows` + `comparator` without needing aggregation (`0a2f4f7`).
724: 
725: **Rule added:** Never add `aggFunc` to a column in the RFQ grid unless you have confirmed that all valueSetters and valueGetters gracefully handle `params.data === undefined` for group rows.

729: ### 2025 — CBM/pc cell edit always reverted on save (first root cause)
730: 
731: **What happened:** Any value typed into CBM/pc (or any column calling `recalculate_Lic_Gen`) disappeared after pressing Enter. The AG Grid cell reverted to empty.
732: 
733: **Root cause (primary):** All 16 margin setter functions in `equation.ts` accessed `params.column.userProvidedColDef.colId`. In AG Grid v35, `userProvidedColDef` is `undefined`, so every call threw a `TypeError`. Because the valueSetter is `async`, AG Grid v35 awaited the rejected Promise and cancelled the edit.
734: 
735: **Root cause (secondary):** `duty_rate.title` hard access (without optional chaining) threw if `duty_rate` was null.

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\rfq\README.md
@@
 ### Fill handle: keep drag-copy, block double-click fill-down
 
 What changed:
 The master RFQ grid still enables AG Grid fill-handle drag-copy through `cellSelection.handle: { mode: 'fill', direction: 'y', setFillValue }`. `rfq.component.ts` also installs `installFillHandleDoubleClickBlocker()` on grid ready, which catches `dblclick` events on `.ag-fill-handle` during the capture phase and stops AG Grid from auto-filling down the grid.
@@
 Future sessions should:
 Do not "fix" this by setting `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or removing `cellSelection.handle`; that breaks drag-copy. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose the fill handle as an on/off feature and do not document a narrower double-click-only setting, so keep the event blocker unless AG Grid adds a first-class option. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag fill and double-click blocking.
+
+### Range copy/paste across RFQ Groups
+
+What changed:
+The master RFQ grid uses AG Grid row grouping for `RFQ Group`, so a dragged cell range can visually cross gray group-header rows. AG Grid's default range-copy path walks displayed row indexes, which includes those group rows. `rfq.component.ts` now provides `sendToClipboard`; it lets AG Grid's default clipboard text pass through for normal ranges, but when a selected range contains group/header/detail/footer rows it rebuilds the copied tab-delimited data from real RFQ item rows only. Paste then lands row-for-row on real item rows instead of being offset by hidden/group rows. The fill-handle path also resolves dropdown values through `findDropdownObject()` first, using the AG Grid `rowNode` as the row-local fallback instead of scanning unrelated `rowData`.
+
+Why:
+Users drag-select down across different RFQ Groups and expect spreadsheet behavior: copied row 1 should paste into target row 1, copied row 2 into target row 2, regardless of group boundaries. Including group rows in the copied rectangle added blank/header rows to the clipboard and made paste appear shifted or mismatched.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Keep `sendToClipboard` scoped to the grouped-row correction. Do not replace AG Grid's clipboard behavior for plain ranges, and do not remove the existing dropdown `processCellForClipboard` / `processCellFromClipboard` conversion. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for grouped range copy, normal range pass-through, and dropdown fill value resolution.
 
 ### Tech Pack Link editing and opening
*** End Patch

+**Why:** Spreadsheet-style copy/paste across RFQ Groups is a normal sourcing workflow. Copying group rows into the clipboard inserted blank/header lines and made paste look offset or mismatched.
+
+**Future sessions should:** Do not remove `sendToClipboard` or "simplify" it back to default AG Grid range copy unless AG Grid adds a documented grouped-range skip option. Keep dropdown copy/paste conversion in `processCellForClipboard` / `processCellFromClipboard`, and keep fill-handle dropdown conversion using `findDropdownObject()` plus the target `rowNode`. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for grouped range copy and dropdown fill resolution.
+
+---
+
 ### "Price for Sales" cells use a native-title tooltip via a cell renderer, not the shared `customTooltip`
